title: Find a 1-cent bug buried deep in an 11-file pricing pipeline
task: `npm test` fails for one case: the widget order total is off by one cent (expected 2645, got 2644). The order-total pipeline spans many small modules (pricing, coupons, discounts, tax, shipping, rounding, currency, inventory, order, ...). Trace the failing total to its ROOT CAUSE and fix it at the source — do not special-case the failing total or paper over it in order.js, and do NOT modify anything under test/. Only one module is actually wrong.
notes: Discriminator: navigation/tracing at scale. The bug is roundDiv in src/rounding.js using Math.floor instead of rounding to nearest (documented half-up); it surfaces only when tax has a fractional cent >= 0.5 (the widget case). Several correct decoy modules (currency/inventory/coupons/discounts/shipping) widen the search. The second check verifies the SOURCE module (rounding.js) actually rounds half-up — catching a downstream paper-over (rounding.js still truncates) WITHOUT false-positiving on a valid Math.floor(x+0.5) idiom (an earlier file_not_contains 'Math.floor' check wrongly failed that correct fix).
